text.title: ASP.NET Core Error Handling, Mapping, and Agent Gotchas
text.summary: ProblemDetails error handling, DTO mapping rules, middleware order, and LLM mistakes to reject.

section.title: Error handling

rule.statement: Use IExceptionHandler (.NET 8+) with AddProblemDetails() and UseExceptionHandler() in Program.cs. Map domain exceptions to RFC 7807 ProblemDetails responses. Never create custom error envelope classes.
rule.rationale: RFC 7807 is the HTTP standard for machine-readable error details. Custom envelopes diverge per endpoint and break API clients that expect the standard format.

section.reference: IExceptionHandler implementation mapping exceptions to RFC 7807

rule.statement: Prefer Mapperly (source-generated) over AutoMapper (reflection-based) for DTO mapping. Mapperly is AOT-compatible and fails at compile time on misconfigured maps.
rule.rationale: AutoMapper mapping misconfigurations fail at runtime (often in production). Mapperly catches mismatches at compile time and has zero runtime overhead.

rule.statement: Never return EF tracked entities from controllers or from public service methods. Always map to a DTO at the service layer.
rule.rationale: Tracked entities serialize with circular references, expose internal columns, and prevent lazy-load exceptions from being caught at the service boundary.

rule.statement: In Program.cs, middleware order must be: UseExceptionHandler → UseHttpsRedirection → UseAuthentication → UseAuthorization → MapControllers. Never reverse UseAuthentication and UseAuthorization.
rule.rationale: UseAuthorization checks ClaimsPrincipal set by UseAuthentication. If UseAuthentication runs after, the principal is always anonymous and [Authorize] is silently skipped.

rule.statement: For multi-step writes (two entity saves + external call), use await using var tx = await db.Database.BeginTransactionAsync(ct); and commit explicitly. Never rely on SaveChanges for cross-aggregate atomicity.
rule.rationale: SaveChanges commits all pending changes in the DbContext at once, but two separate SaveChanges calls in the same request are NOT atomic without an explicit transaction.

rule.statement: Reject diffs that inject DbContext into a singleton service constructor. Require IServiceProvider + CreateScope() or change service registration to Scoped.
rule.rationale: Captive dependency: DbContext is scoped; singleton holds it for the process lifetime, causing state corruption under concurrent requests.

rule.statement: Reject diffs that add Result<T> wrappers in services when the project uses exception-based error handling. Do not mix the two patterns in one codebase.
rule.rationale: Mixing exception-based and Result-based error handling creates dual error paths; controllers must handle both, and inconsistency spreads.

rule.statement: Reject diffs with tests using MockBehavior.Default that don't assert call counts. Require MockBehavior.Strict or integration tests with Testcontainers.
rule.rationale: MockBehavior.Default silently returns default values for unconfigured calls, allowing tests to pass even when the production code calls wrong methods.
